purpose: Defines the !context {topic} slash command — performs a deep, read-only context gather about a feature/domain in the target project. Has dual-mode execution: when {project_root}/.synapse/knowledge/manifest.json exists (PKI bootstrapped), it queries domain_index, tag_index, and concept_map for instant routing then reads matched annotations; otherwise falls back to grep/glob search. PKI data is additive — it never replaces grep, only enriches it. Outputs a structured Files-by-Area / Architecture / Connections / Key Observations summary, with an extra PKI Context section (Gotchas, Patterns, Relationships) when annotations contributed. Always verifies candidates and flags stale annotations.
gotchas: PKI is additive, NOT required — command must work identically when no PKI exists. Always proceed to Step 2 (grep/glob) even after PKI hits.
Stale annotations (stale: true in manifest) must be flagged AND the actual file must be re-read — annotation may be outdated.
PKI tags/domains feed back into Step 2 search terms — annotations may surface terms you wouldn't have guessed.
Coverage gaps: files found by grep/glob but NOT in PKI must be flagged as unannotated (new/recent files).
If PKI coverage <50% on relevant files, suggest !learn_update; if PKI absent entirely, suggest !learn at the bottom of the output.
Output must fit ~50-150 lines — extract facts, do not dump file contents.
patterns: Dual-mode workflow: PKI-fast-path → grep/glob fallback → merge → enriched summary.
Term expansion: original topic → synonyms → likely identifiers → cast wide net before filtering.
Annotation-driven traversal: use relationships array (serves/consumes/mirrors/extends) to trace connections without re-reading files.
conventions: Output structure differs based on PKI availability (with-PKI version has extra PKI Context section).
Section structure: # title → Overview → Usage → Execution Steps → Rules.
